place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Fitness First One George is my favorite branch by far. The space is beautifully well-lit in the mornings, with a great view that really sets the mood for a good workout. You can even get a bit of sun without being bothered, which is a nice bonus.

The staff, Yogesh and her team,  trainers and cleaners are consistently friendly and approachable, and the place is always kept tidy. Locker rooms are clean and well-maintained, making the overall experience comfortable from start to finish.

The location is also very convenient. Just know your timings, and you’ll have a great workout every time. Definitely a branch I keep coming back to.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
This studio has a nice view and even gets good sunlight—perfect if you enjoy a little post-workout suntanning.

The equipment selection is complete. The floor plan is a bit small, but during off-peak hours it feels comfortable and easy to move around.
Shower and sauna areas are clean and well maintained.

The personal trainers are polite and considerate of members who train on their own.

Shout out to Dasha and the front desk team for always being friendly and welcoming. They really set the tone when you walk in!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Workout are always creative and challenging but what I most value is the excellent impact for every minute spent exercising. I have spent longer in the gym but with nothing like the positive result, but Dasha helped keep me focused, sharp and in shape. I have loved training with Dasha. She is a very professional, encouraging and all-round lovely person to work with.....✌️
I enjoy coming to my sessions and, with her help, discovering what I can do next.
Anyone wanting a truly dedicated PT - Dasha will do such an amazing job.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Awesome gym, awesome staff, awesome trainers.
Love the bathroom too as I’m very particular abt bathroom.
Special shout out to Ramp, Marlon, Yogesh, Dasha, Nithia, and all staff that made my experience at the gym a great one!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs

Their positive attitude created a comfortable atmosphere, and they were always ready to assist with any questions. Excellent customer service and a great first impression of the gym.

review_text:
Clarke Quay Central is a great mall with a fantastic view of the Boat Quay riverside 🌊🚤✨. It has a wide variety of shops to explore, and you can even find some discounted items at the first-floor lobby—perfect for a little bargain shopping 🛍️💸.
What I love most is its convenient location 📍. After shopping, you can easily walk over to Boat Quay, Fort Canning Park, the National Museum of Singapore, and other nearby attractions. It’s a nice spot to relax, shop, and enjoy the scenic surroundings all in one place 🌿🏙️.
Overall, a convenient and enjoyable place to visit if you’re in the area 👍😊

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: It has been a while since I was last here - at this, in the Chinatown area, shopping mall that is patronised by locals and tourists alike. While I have always found the layout and connectivity inside the building to be at times, somewhat "confusing", I can see that it is still fairly popular - helped by a Don Don Donki and its food stalls at its basement level one. In fact, I was here this morning to check out its recently (?) opened food court - Sinfoodie, and I was impressed by the choice of food stalls thereat and the dining area; although admittedly, I was there well before the peak lunch hour and I did wonder what it would look like then. Anyway, I see myself returning to this shopping mall for Sinfoodie.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries

The placement of shops and odd corners often cuts off the natural flow of foot traffic, making spontaneous discovery quite a challenge. It isn't the best place for a casual stroll if you’re looking to explore new stores.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Clarke Quay Central is a vibrant mall popular with both tourists and locals, thanks to its prime location next to the nightlife scene along the Singapore River.
The mall offers a good mix of cafes and restaurants, with some dining spots featuring scenic river views.

In the basement, you’ll also find the bustling Japanese supermarket Don Don Donki, which is a draw on its own.
Conveniently situated near several hotels and directly above Clarke Quay MRT station, it’s especially lively and atmospheric from the evening onwards, when the riverside lights come alive.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Went down last week to Taiyo Bread Shop at level 3 for a afternoon tea break. The cake is sweet & delicious. Price reasonable for this area. Environment is quiet for chill & relax for solo ranger cafe hopper like me 😃

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A lifesaver in the heat of Chinatown! Located right above the MRT station, this mall is the perfect place to cool down. I highly recommend visiting the NTUC FairPrice supermarket in the basement to buy souvenirs (chocolates, coffee) at local prices instead of tourist prices outside. Plenty of good dining options too. Clean restrooms and very accessible.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: I really enjoy walking around here, the streets are very walkable, clean. There are so many food spots, cafe, and small shops, so it never feels boring. Easy to access by MRT and a great place to just stroll, eat, and enjoy the atmosphere, especially in the evening.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Chinatown has the old side that has not changed and the new side with full of contemporary restaurants. In the center is a beautiful temple. Along the streets are full of shops selling souvenirs, food, fruits, etc. At night, it is totally different when it is lights come on. The famous street food place called Hawkers market is also located in Chinatown. Had a wonderful time there.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Chinatown is one of the most lively and colorful places in Singapore. It beautifully blends traditional culture with modern city life. The streets are full of energy, with souvenir shops, local markets, and amazing food options everywhere.

The area is very clean and well maintained, making it easy and comfortable to explore on foot. You can visit famous landmarks like the Sri Mariamman Temple and Buddha Tooth Relic Temple, which add a strong cultural and spiritual charm to the place.

Chinatown is also a paradise for food lovers. From street food to authentic Chinese restaurants, every corner offers something delicious. It’s a perfect place for shopping, photography, and experiencing Singapore’s heritage. A must-visit destination for anyone traveling to Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This is the newest and best shopping mall in Chinatown precinct with Fairprice supermarket and many F&B shops without the quaint massage parlors or antique shops. There is even a library that offers rich heritage resources and comfortable resting place for both locals and tourists.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
​If you're looking for the most scenic library in Singapore, this is it. Located on the 3rd floor of VivoCity, library@harbourfront is a masterclass in modern, functional design.
​Why it’s a must-visit for me.  The floor-to-ceiling windows offer a breathtaking, panoramic view of the Sentosa waterfront and the cable cars. Snagging one of the yellow deck chairs facing the water is a total game-changer.
​
Despite being in one of Singapore’s busiest malls, it is remarkably peaceful and quiet. The "coastal theme" with its undulating ceilings and blue-green hues makes the entire space feel like a sanctuary.
​
I love the clear distinction between the Adults/Teens zone and the Children's area. It keeps the reading lounges tranquil while the kids have their own interactive "Tinker Truck" and AR books to explore.

It's modern tech, highly efficient with digital newspaper stations, "Learning Pods" for TED Talks, and an easy Grab-n-Go system.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Visited Library @ HarbourFront — one of the nicest local libraries to relax and enjoy some quiet time with a book.
The view overlooking Sentosa Island adds to the calming ambiance, making it a great spot to unwind.

It’s mostly a self-service library with everything fully automated, from borrowing to returning books.
The setup is modern and efficient, making the whole experience smooth and convenient.
A great place for both readers and those looking for a peaceful retreat.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Really love the library in the middle of a crowded mall. Very happy they have a security gantry to allow people to scan passes to enter. Divided session of young kids and other section is although a hassle but necessary because the children session can be a little noisy. Also love they have the self check out gantry which is cool.

Books selection may not have the widest variety but good enough for town. I find limited seatings among bookshelves area although proper table/seats are scattered all around. I find a lot of people occupied those seats for working, studying or leisure reading. I really don't like the people taking advantage to nap there but the librarian did go around stopping them. The other point that was not pleasant are the seats facing the waterfront. While it is definitely such a good experience reading and facing sentosa, the air was really stuffy and not ventilated. The heat from the glass was intense and the air con wasn't strong enough.

Beautifully furnished library.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
The Singapore Recreation Club (SRC)
Review on 20/1/2026

The Singapore Recreation Club (SRC), founded in 1883 and set along the iconic Padang, is one of Singapore’s most historic social‑sports institutions. Its clubhouse offers a wide range of sports, recreation, dining, and event facilities that continue to serve a vibrant and diverse community.

The club’s most striking feature is its grand entrance, framed by two sweeping curved staircases. This graceful, ceremonial design creates a sense of ascending into a place of heritage and dignity. Against the backdrop of the Padang and the Civic District, the entrance stands out as a timeless and memorable architectural landmark.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Private club in central district with roofed warm and cold swimming pool, restaurant, bar with live band and cafe.
Cost environment for private gathering and easily accessible by MRT via City Hall station

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Dinner with an old friend at the SRC'S Chinese Restaurant. Reminiscent of the old days in Singapore, with traditions alive and well. Very pleasant evening 😊😋

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Our family had a wonderful visit to the Singapore Recreation Club (SRC) during their open house! The location right by the Padang is fantastic, and we were impressed that the facilities are truly top-notch. The pool looked inviting, and the entire club was clean and prestigious. It's clear why it's a desirable spot, but be aware that it is indeed for members only. We really enjoyed our temporary visit and appreciated seeing the high quality of the club.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Not a member but, their facility is quite complete. From dance, restaurant, chill drinking club, outside chill area, car parking, swimming pool, gym, to pickleball court

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Clarke Quay is one of the MRT station of North East Line. It is located just next to the southern part of Singapore River and Clarke Quay, underneath The Central building. It is also within walking distance to Chinatown. This station is also serves by bus stops which may bring passengers to different parts of Singapore.

It was located at underground level with lifts and escalators leading down from the ground level. Toilets were available for commuters. Signages were numerous and useful to assist travellers. to the right path for boarding the train or even exiting the station. This station was clean, comfortable and had well air circulation.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Superb MRT station...
Neat & clean...
Every 2 minutes have metro train timing...
Simply grocery item's are available here...
Like 7 eleven etc....
Very nice experience...

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Singapore's MRT is the standard by which the rest of the world needs to catch up. Incredibly well designed, efficient, timely, well maintained and clean. So easy to use it's a tourists dream for getting around Singapore. You don't need a special ticket or card to use it, a credit card or cash card is all you need to tap on and tap off and the cost is cheaper than anything else we've seen; certainly much cheaper than Australia or Europe.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: So full of lighted lanes and structures at night. In a restaurant called wings bar, happy hour is from 5pm-8pm and all drinks are 50% off during that time. Saw a lot of bars and restaurants with the same. Not much crowded, maybe due to Covid. Could eat decently for around 100SGD for 2 people

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
From Clarke Quay MRT (Exit 5)
You can walk to Chainatown Point Taxi Stand that have Chinatown MRT Station below

Also from Chinatown MRT Station Exit (3)
Near OG Building you can actually walk to
Outram Park MRT Exit (3)

Save your money for three stops by walking 👌🏼

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: A very good way to visit a number of attractions in one go. They have two stops and allow customers to take off. Full ride takes around 49 minutes. Encourage to take the ride early evening to maximize the enjoyment and capture the beauty of Singapore lights.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: The 40 minute cruise met my expectations. The boat tour provided an excellent overview of the city's landmarks, complete with fascinating historical narration. The traditional bumboats, beautifully refurbished for modern sightseeing, were a highlight themselves. It's a fantastic way to get a feel of the city's cultural transformation to the impressive buoyant, thriving city it has become. If possible do this before exploring each landmark individually. 🚢🚤

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
as a singaporean, can use sg culture pass! not a bad experience to do with kids and older folks. the best part was waving to all the people walking and sitting along the river hehehe felt so youthful.

40 minutes of calm chill bumboat riding experience. people will snatch the seats at the back cause there has no shelter and is the nicest to take photos. we did the 7.10pm to 7.45pm timing for the sunset (but cannot see lol) so it was nice to see the day turn into dusk.

id say the counter service can be more clear on the ride haha. thought the ride has fixed timings but there arent. the boats come about every 15 minutes and you just queue up. also didnt know that you can drop off at mbs!!! why never tell us!

inside the tour, the audio recorded tour guide is only in english and not loud enough for those seated at the back.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: A must do when in Singapore. Our group did it while waiting for our hotel rooms to be ready. We had the boat to ourselves. Many were pleasantly surprised when we went out into the marina (we had come from Merlion). This cruise provides a very nice view from a different side. And a nice audio history of the area. Let the little dude take your picture, he plays some nice camera tricks creating interesting pics. If I ever make it back, will do this at night.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: That's a great experience that allows visitors different views of the most gorgeus parts of Singapore. The cruise lasts about 44 minutes and they have a more expensive time slot that coincides with the light show at the Marina Bay. Ideally, starting the cruise just before sunset allows you to experience different views of the skyline. It is a no frills boat, with an automated narration,but it feels safe and well managed. Definitely a must do experience in Singapore.

review_text:
Really enjoyed visiting VivoCity 🛍️
The mall is HUGE and has pretty much everything you need. Lots of shopping, food options, cafes, and entertainment all in one place. It’s also super convenient since it connects directly to Sentosa, which makes it a great stop before or after exploring the island 🌴🚆

The mall houses two floors for those who enjoy shopping.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Visited the National Library Building mainly for The Albatross File: Singapore’s Independence Declassified exhibition, and it turned out to be a meaningful experience. The exhibition itself is relatively small, but the historical significance is immense. It provides a fascinating and candid look into the events leading up to Singapore’s separation from Malaysia in 1965, including documents and notes showing that both sides had, by then, come to see separation as the likely path forward. Seeing the actual letters, transcripts, and handwritten notes made this pivotal chapter of Singapore’s history feel very real and personal

I also took some time to roam around the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library and was impressed by how incredibly well managed the entire place is. The building itself is architecturally striking, modern, spacious, and filled with natural light. The library environment feels calm, organized, and conducive for learning and research, with clear signage and well-maintained facilities throughout

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The National Library is conveniently located with good traffic access from around the island. It’s a comfy cool place to be if your want to study, research and just reading. You may spent hours but no worries about hunger as the are many choices of food in the area.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The Albatross File exhibition at NLB is a fascinating showcase of Singapore’s separation history, featuring declassified documents, oral histories, and immersive multimedia displays. It brings to life the tense years leading up to independence, blending archival records with dramatized storytelling.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
2026 January - Did quick look around, enjoyed reading the stories from the mini exhibition Knots of Time. Looks like there are other exhibitions and all sorts of collection, will be back to explore and read.

Great ambience, nice for researchers and the public alike. Place has plenty of space if you'd like to get some work done, offers brilliant views, and even has a skygarden. Power and water is readily available, and theres even sufficient space for a stroll if you're feeling a mental block coming on.

Will be back to check out the albatross files. The atrium at the bottom is very pleasant, and the vaguely 'heritage' old chang kee seems like a good place to check out as well.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Albatross exhibition of Singapore

Thankful for this curated history that bares all that Singaporeans may not know. We had the privilege of having seniors in their 70s who came along, cementing our understanding of what they had gone thru.

The whole setup allowed us to go at our own pace and the voice of our late and retired ministers add a sense of urgency and realism.

A suggestion for those who are not English educated, as the ministers are speaking in English, please allow translator set made avail in various language to our non English speaking seniors.

Lastly the wooden board is super heavy. Made it very difficult for us to lift up the board to read the files.  Lastly is there a need to have the board hidden? Most don’t know it exist until someone lifted it up.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Gardens by the Bay is truly an amazing place and definitely a must-visit attraction in Singapore. The entire area is beautifully designed, extremely clean, and very well maintained, making it a wonderful experience for visitors of all ages.

The Flower Dome and Cloud Forest offer a unique opportunity to see plants and flowers from different parts of the world. During our visit, we also experienced the Tulip Feast, where we saw many beautiful varieties of tulips in full bloom. The colorful display was absolutely stunning and added a vibrant touch to the overall experience. The indoor waterfall inside the Cloud Forest is especially impressive and creates a refreshing atmosphere. The Supertree Grove is another major highlight, and the night light show there feels absolutely magical with the music and colorful lights.

The whole place is spacious and easy to explore, although there is a lot to walk around, so it can feel a little tiring after some time. Still, the beautiful views and attractions make the walking completely worth it.

There are many perfect photo spots throughout the gardens, especially during the evening when the night view looks stunning. Souvenir shops are also available if you would like to buy gifts or memories from your visit.

Overall, it’s a peaceful and beautiful place to relax, enjoy nature, and experience one of Singapore’s most iconic attractions. Highly recommended, especially for first-time visitors.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Gardens by the Bay is truly a highlight of Singapore and absolutely not to be missed. I’d highly recommend getting a combo ticket for the Cloud Forest and Flower Dome—plan for about 2-3 hours to cover both.

The Cloud Forest is spectacular, almost Jurassic Park-like in parts, and enjoyable for both kids and adults. There’s misting at specific times, so it’s worth checking those in advance. A great way to explore is to head to the top level first and then make your way down, stopping at the different exhibits along the way.
The Flower Dome is equally beautiful, showcasing flowers and plants from around the world, with plenty of photo-worthy corners throughout. There’s also a nice gift shop with garden and flower-themed souvenirs.

I also got a separate ticket for the OCBC Skyway and it was 100% worth it. I went up right at 7:45 PM during the light and sound show and got to watch it from the top—an incredible experience. You can stay up for about 15 minutes, and while there’s an elevator, lines to go can get long.

For food, the Jurassic-themed food court nearby is a good option for dinner. Overall, it felt like a well-spent ~$60 to explore all three places. I’d suggest going after 3 PM and spending the entire evening there to fully enjoy the whole area.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Gardens by the Bay is absolutely magical and one of my favorite spots in Singapore. The Supertree Grove is stunning, especially at night when the light and sound show begins—it feels like stepping into a fairy tale. I loved walking through the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest; both are beautifully maintained and full of unique plants and flowers from around the world. The pathways and gardens are perfect for leisurely strolls, and there are so many spots to take photos or just pause and enjoy the scenery. It’s peaceful, inspiring, and feels like a green oasis right in the middle of the city. Whether you’re a nature lover, a photography enthusiast, or just looking for a relaxing escape, Gardens by the Bay is a must-visit.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
During my recent visit to Singapore, one of the most memorable places I explored was Gardens by the Bay. This remarkable attraction is a perfect example of how nature, technology, and sustainability can come together to create something truly extraordinary.

From the moment I entered the gardens, I was impressed by the sheer scale and beauty of the place. The landscape is meticulously maintained, featuring a stunning variety of plants, flowers, and themed gardens from around the world. The iconic Supertree Grove is undoubtedly the highlight, with its futuristic structures creating a breathtaking skyline, especially during the evening when they are beautifully illuminated.

I also had the opportunity to visit the conservatories, where the climate-controlled environments showcase unique plant species from different regions. Walking through these attractions felt like traveling across multiple ecosystems in a single day. The attention to detail, cleanliness, and visitor-friendly facilities were outstanding.

What impressed me most was Singapore’s commitment to environmental sustainability, which is reflected throughout the entire garden. Despite being located in the heart of a modern city, the gardens provide a peaceful escape where visitors can relax, take photographs, and appreciate nature.

Whether you are traveling with family, friends, or exploring solo, Gardens by the Bay is a must-visit destination. It offers a unique blend of natural beauty, innovation, and world-class design that leaves a lasting impression. My visit was truly unforgettable, and I would highly recommend it to anyone planning a trip to Singapore.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Gardens by the Bay is one of Singapore’s most impressive attractions and a place that keeps rewarding repeat visits. What started as a quick visit to the Supertrees has grown into an entire day of exploration, from the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest to the outdoor gardens, lakes, sculptures, and hidden corners scattered throughout the grounds

The Cloud Forest remains the highlight for me. Walking through the cool mist, towering ferns, orchids, and prehistoric-looking plants feels like stepping into another world. The elevated walkways offer fantastic perspectives, and the constantly changing displays make every visit feel fresh. The Flower Dome is equally beautiful, with seasonal floral exhibitions and meticulously maintained landscapes

Beyond the conservatories, there is so much to discover. The Supertrees are iconic, the outdoor gardens are peaceful and surprisingly diverse, and even simple walks around the lakes reveal interesting plants, wildlife, and photo opportunities. I particularly enjoyed wandering through areas that many visitors overlook, including the lily-covered ponds and the themed garden displays

Another pleasant surprise was discovering that the Conservatory Gift Shop leads into a complimentary Jurassic World Experience, which added an unexpected element of fun to the visit. Combined with convenient access via MRT, dining options such as Satay by the Bay, excellent facilities, and world-class landscaping, it is easy to spend an entire day here without getting bored

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
If you are around Little India area, City Square Mall is actually a pretty nice place to chill and makan.
It's just a short walk from Mustafa Centre and the mall has a good mix of food spots, from quick bites to proper restaurants and food court options. Halal food choices are quite easy to find too.

Shopping wise, it's the kind of mall that feels practical and comfortable. Got everyday stores like Uniqlo and Decathlon, so you can actually shop without everything feeling overly expensive.

Bonus point for tea lovers… theres Beutea too🧋

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. There were many willing to pay and relax on the chairs here and that’s good.

Latest update! A wonderful discovery and if you’re looking for a barber nearby this mall, there’s one! Jerudili Barber and it’s operated by a passionate with professionalism guy. He takes care of your hair well and really comfortable. He cuts hair great too! Please do support since it’s quite new. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Chinatown MRT Station (NE4/DT19) is a vibrant and centrally located transit hub in Singapore, serving the North-East Line (NEL) and Downtown Line (DTL). Situated in the heart of Chinatown, it provides easy access to one of Singapore’s most culturally rich and historic neighborhoods.

The station features eye-catching design elements that reflect the heritage of Chinatown, with artwork and motifs inspired by traditional Chinese culture. Its proximity to iconic attractions like the Chinatown Heritage Centre, Buddha Tooth Relic Temple, and Sri Mariamman Temple makes it a popular stop for both tourists and locals.

Chinatown MRT is surrounded by a variety of dining, shopping, and entertainment options, including hawker stalls, souvenir shops, and boutique stores. It’s also well-connected, offering seamless transfers between the NEL and DTL, making it a convenient starting point for exploring other parts of Singapore.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Watching Commonwealth MRT Station rise from the ground years ago is a memory etched in grit, defined by enduring endless clouds of construction dust and grinding gridlock along Commonwealth Avenue. It required immense patience from the neighborhood, but seeing that sleek, elevated station finally open felt like a hard-won victory for the community. It completely transformed daily commutes, seamlessly linking our quiet enclave directly to the rest of Singapore.
The landscape around this familiar transit landmark continues to evolve rapidly. The two primary schools and one secondary school that once framed the station—filling the morning air with the bright laughter and energy of generations of students—have now been demolished. Seeing those familiar buildings vanish feels deeply poignant, marking the bittersweet end of a cherished chapter in local history.
In their place, the land is being cleared to make way for high-rise public housing, injecting fresh life and new families into this mature estate. While it is tough to witness the nostalgic landmarks of our youth fade away, Commonwealth MRT Station stands firm as an anchor through it all, evolving from a disruptive construction site into the vital, reliable spine of a constantly renewing neighborhood.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

Woodlands Waterfront Park is one of my favorite spots to unwind. The park offers a beautiful, relaxing view of the Johor Strait, especially during sunset. The waterfront promenade is perfect for a peaceful stroll or a bike ride, and there’s plenty of open space for picnics with family and friends.

I also love the iconic jetty—it’s a great place to take in the sea breeze and watch the boats go by. The park is well-maintained, with clean paths and plenty of benches to sit and enjoy the scenery. Highly recommended for anyone looking to escape the city hustle without going too far!

review_text:
Paragon has been a familiar hangout spot for me for many years, and it’s still one of Orchard Road’s most dependable malls. It has a classy, spacious feel and remains a great place to spend time whether you’re shopping, meeting friends, or just taking a slow walk indoors

There’s a strong mix of retail stores, good eateries, and practical conveniences all under one roof. I also like that it has Cold Storage Gold / Fresh for quality groceries, which makes it more than just a shopping mall. The presence of medical clinics adds to the convenience too

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This is a modern upscale 6 level shopping mall right in the heart of Orchard Road. Most people who shop here knows what they want and they just go straight to the shop. Others come here for their lunch or simply have tea here for nice chit chats. Now’s the time for Chinese New Year shopping so the mall is beautifully decorated this year !! I love the beautiful lights !!! And can feel the Chinese New Year vibes and mood !!! I came here to buy my CNY clothes at Metro Paragon on level two. It’s been a wonderful and fruitful trip !! Bought 6 pieces 👍👍👍💗💗💗

review_text: A wonderful and good shopping experience having good brands like Prada and Gucci in them. It also has amazing food and restaurant options on the basement level. The best food you could find is the fish and Coast company which serves authentic fish to perfection and also the area has some good Italian food joints, and some nice Asian and local cuisine as well. It also has a nice supermarket to buy daily essentials and fresh fruits.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Stepping into Paragon feels like entering a high-end gallery. The layout is spacious and intuitive, featuring polished marble floors and a glass-fronted facade that has become a landmark on Orchard Road.

​Key Highlights
-​Luxury Fashion: The mall is synonymous with high fashion, housing iconic multi-story flagships for Gucci, Prada, and Givenchy, alongside luxury heavyweights like Saint Laurent, Balenciaga, and Loewe.

-​The "Junior" Floor: Level 5 is a dedicated sanctuary for families, featuring high-end children’s labels like Armani Junior and Petit Bateau, complemented by a free indoor playground and top-tier enrichment centers.

-​Exquisite Dining: From the Michelin-starred Crystal Jade Golden Palace to the legendary Imperial Treasure Super Peking Duck, the culinary offerings lean heavily toward quality. For something more casual yet chic, PS.Cafe and the basement's Din Tai Fung are perennial favorites.

-​Medical Wellness: Uniquely, the mall is connected to Paragon Medical, a premium tower housing world-class specialists and aesthetic clinics, allowing visitors to combine wellness appointments with luxury retail.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Paragon Shopping Centre is one of my favourite malls along Orchard Road when I feel like doing some upscale shopping. I enjoy browsing the luxury brands here—it’s elegant but still comfortable to walk around. The basement has plenty of nice food choices too, whether it’s grabbing a quick bite or sitting down for a proper meal. I also like that the mall has a supermarket and even a medical wing with specialist clinics, so it’s really convenient for almost everything.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Clarke Quay is one of the most energetic and colorful areas in Singapore, especially in the evening. The riverside setting, combined with the bright neon lights and lively atmosphere, makes it a fantastic place to walk around and enjoy the city at night.
There are plenty of restaurants, bars, and entertainment options, ranging from casual dining to upscale spots with beautiful waterfront views. The colorful canopy overhead adds a unique architectural touch and helps keep the area comfortable even in warmer weather.
Whether you're looking for a relaxed dinner by the river, live music, or just a scenic stroll along the Singapore River, Clarke Quay delivers. It’s clean, well organized, and feels safe — perfect for couples, friends, or even families earlier in the evening.
Highly recommended if you want to experience Singapore’s nightlife in a vibrant and picturesque setting.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I visited Clarke Quay during the day and found it to be a really charming place. It offers a wide variety of restaurants, making it a great spot to explore different dining options all in one area. The colorful buildings and riverside setting give it a very cute and vibrant atmosphere.

Although I went during the daytime, I can imagine it becomes even more lively and visually appealing at night, when all the venues are open and the lights reflect beautifully along the river. Definitely a place worth visiting, especially in the evening for the full experience.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Clarke Quay is a vibrant riverside destination that’s very popular with tourists, offering a huge selection of bars and restaurants for nightlife and dining. The atmosphere really picks up in the evening with the music and light. It is a great spot whether you're looking for light bites or a full night out.

As a local, I appreciate that it’s well maintained and clean. It is a good place for an evening stroll or leisurely walk. While it feels touristy and the prices are definitely on the higher side, it’s still a solid spot to soak in the city’s energy and enjoy a wide variety of food and drink in one location.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Clarke Quay is one of those places in Singapore that truly comes alive after sunset. Set along the river, the area buzzes with neon lights, upbeat music, and a constant flow of people soaking in the atmosphere. Lined with a wide mix of bars, pubs, clubs, and restaurants, it offers something for everyone—whether you’re in the mood for a relaxed drink by the water or a high-energy night out with loud beats and dancing crowds.

The riverside setting adds a charming touch, making evening walks especially enjoyable. You can stroll along the promenade, watch boats glide past, and take in the colorful reflections dancing on the water. There’s always something happening—live music spilling out of venues, street performers adding flair, and groups of friends laughing their way from one spot to another.

Yes, it does feel a little touristy, with busy crowds and popular chains, but that’s part of its appeal. The energy is infectious, the vibe is festive, and the options are endless. If you’re looking for nightlife, people-watching, and a fun place to unwind after dark, Clarke Quay delivers the experience with style and spirit

So happy to come across this affordable makan place, just next to Clarke Quay!

The Minced Meat Mushroom noodle was really good! The noodle was nicely cooked, not soggy nor hard, and the sauces mixed really well with it. The mushroom has good vinegar taste. Meat was nicely cooked and tender. Soup was tasty too.
(Pic only shows half of what is left. Too excited to eat!)

The one I most appreciated was the staff at the drink stall. Very polite and patient. So rare to have someone like him as almost all drink stalls staff are usually quite rude and always rushing the customers.

Visited: 1 April 2025 around 9.30pm

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Parked at Clarke Quay CP by Canning Lane, in the early morning I wasn't sure where I could get some simple breakfast or should I walk over to The Central mall. But I walked along the canning lane road and turned right, facing Fort Canning Park, found SG Hawker a 24 hours opened place just right at the corner. The toast set was only $4.50! The toast was crispy and the butter was thinly melted. A great find for me.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Located near the river front they offer a wide variety of curry rice options.
Ambience - place is pretty hygienic and they offer outdoor sitting and it is situated near the river front .
Food - Quality and quantity of food they offer is great, we got there fried prawns curry rice and fried chicken curry rice both tasted really awesome.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
SG Hawker is a place which has many local dishes like Fried Hokkien Mee, Hainanese Curry Rice, Laksa, Fried Kway Teow, etc

It's located near Fort Canning and Clarke Quay Mrt.

Though it's not air conditioned, there's many fans around to keep us cool.

Interior design is cool and trendy. There's also different types of chairs and tables. There are cushioned, metal and wooden chairs. There's indoor and outdoor seats which is along the road.

I bought the 85 Redhill mini wok. It's very nice and affordable.

#ILoveSGHawker

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: We had our morning food here and the place is beside Clark Quay area near the road. The place is good and is open not that crowded. The food sold were also nice and good.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
We visited Maxwell Food Centre at around 8:00 AM on a Monday, so it was still relatively quiet with very few queues. Unfortunately, the famous chicken rice stall was closed, which was a bit disappointing. This was actually my second visit, and I still haven't had the chance to try it!

Thankfully, there are plenty of other stalls serving chicken rice, so we simply picked one without a line and it didn't disappoint. In fact, that's the beauty of Maxwell Food Centre. There are so many food options to choose from, and everything we tried was delicious. The drinks were great too, and the prices are very affordable. Those in the pictures are only some of those we found really delicious.

Whether you're craving local favorites or just looking for a good meal, Maxwell Food Centre never disappoints. If you're in Singapore, make sure to stop by for delicious food at budget-friendly prices. Just keep in mind that if you're hoping to try the famous chicken rice, it's worth checking the stall's opening hours before you go!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There’s a wide variety of food and the laksa was absolutely amazing. It’s cheaper if you pay cash instead of card. The staffs in charge of cleaning the trays were really proactive. We didn’t try the super crowded stalls, but the ones we had were pretty solid in terms of quality. Also no bad odor

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
I was here in the evening time and most of the stalls were closed for the day.
But I am very fortunate of finding a chicken rice shop still open.
Uncle Louis Famous chicken rice. Uncle was very hands on. He takes each customer's order attentively and gives suggestions along the way.
I ordered chicken with soup and veggies. Very tender, fresh veggie sides and hot rice. Yummy! Welcome local dinner on my 1st night in Singapore.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A step back in time with fantastic food variety at really bargain prices. Only one of the 3 stall rows has high velocity fans for extra cooling. A few obvious winners among the vendors. We had Vietnamese  Tofu and noodles and vegetarian rice paper rolls.
A local character has found a way to always get his favorite seat - Xtra load rap music. He managed to get a whole table under a fan for himself during the lunch peak. There's someone who understands marketing!
A fantastic authentic street food location that has a busy lunch peak but is much quieter otherwise.
The lady selling fresh crushed fruit and sugar can drinks is a standout nearthe China Town exit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: My favourite eating place in the city by far. So much to choose from, foodie heaven. There's always a good selection of shops open. Can be very busy at time with queues for the most popular places.  Don't be scared to share tables. When finished, put your tray in designated area before leaving.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
I had a great experience with my personal coaching training. My coach Henry had helped me improve my strength and build muscle, especially my shoulder strength, which has improved significantly. My coach provided personalized guidance, including home workout routines that made it easier to stay consistent.

The gym environment is also a big plus, it’s a boutique gym with more privacy compared to typical commercial gyms with memberships. If you’re looking for a private training environment and a dedicated trainer, I highly recommend looking for Henry at Revolt.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: This is the first time I started using trainer as i take a plunge into my fitness journey and I couldnt have chosen an better person than Hans. He balances intensity and fun very well and is also mindful that he doesnt push me into full exhaustion during the session. The Gym is also very well managed (apart from odd days of no hot water), clean and i never had to wait for my turn to use any of the facilities/gym equipment.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Shasha is an outstanding trainer. After a long break from exercise, she helped me rebuild a consistent routine with perfect pacing and endless encouragement. Her focus on form and adaptability made me feel safe and motivated. I’ve regained my confidence and actually enjoy working out again! Highly recommend, especially if you're getting back into fitness.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
I’ve had a great experience training at Revolt, and have already noticed my changes. My mindset toward exercise has improved significantly, and I’ve genuinely started to enjoy exercising.

My trainer Henry has created training plans tailored to my level /goals and has also provided helpful diet / nutrition advice. I’m very grateful for the guidance and encouragement, and would recommend Revolt and Henry to anyone looking to improve their fitness in a positive and sustainable way.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: I’ve been training with Hans once a week over the past few months and what I appreciate most is his focus on proper form and technique, which has helped me train safely and effectively. He takes a realistic, sustainable approach to fitness and works with my individual goals rather than pushing unrealistic expectations.
